narrative.headline: 2022 Tesla Model X: steering leads the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 121 complaints with NHTSA. Steering is the most reported area at 34%, followed by forward collision avoidance (17%) and the electrical system (12%). No deaths and no injuries are reported in the top areas except forward collision avoidance (1). A few areas tie to crashes or fire, so review the details below.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 41 reports (34%), linked to 3 crashes and no injuries. Pay attention to steering feel, especially at low speed, on a test drive.

issues.sample: Vehicle repeatedly applied the brakes while on cruise control. This sometimes seemed to be a response to other vehicles approaching in the oncoming traffic lane, but sometimes seemed totally unexpected. Once, the vehicle even sounded the collision alarm when no collision danger w
issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 20 reports (17%), tied to 1 crash and 1 injury. Owners describe unexpected braking, so test the driver-assist behavior.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 15 reports (12%), including 2 with fire and 1 crash. The fire count makes this worth checking carefully.

issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: 12 reports (10%) but the most crashes of any area, with 5. No injuries reported. Worth a close look given the crash count.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: 10 reports (8%), with 1 fire and no crashes or injuries. Owners mention restraint-system fault warnings; check for any dashboard alerts.
